question.text: For grievances related to the National Pension System (NPS), the correct escalation order is:

answer.text: CRA (NSDL e-Governance) → NPS Trust (30 days) → PFRDA Ombudsman
Point of Presence → SEBI → PFRDA Ombudsman

explanation.text: **The Logic:** NPS grievance escalation follows the order: CRA (Central Recordkeeping Agency, which is NSDL e-Governance) → NPS Trust (if unresolved within 30 days) → PFRDA Ombudsman. The CRA is the first point of contact for NPS-related grievances. **The Trap:** Reversing CRA and NPS Trust in the escalation order is the most common mistake. SEBI has no jurisdiction over NPS — it is regulated by PFRDA. Candidates may also confuse NPS grievance mechanisms with securities market complaint processes. **Cross-Exam Context:** NPS grievance mechanisms are tested in NISM Series X-A and NISM NPS (Retirement Adviser) certification exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** NSDL e-Governance as the CRA is the starting point for all NPS operational issues. Keep the 30-day window in mind when escalating to NPS Trust — premature escalation will be returned.
